updateMessage: Adds experimental France 2026 resident tax rules, including quotient-familial approximations, employee payroll deductions, and taxable securities wrappers.

incomeTax.name: Impôt sur le revenu
incomeTax.tooltip: Resident France ordinary-income approximation using the 2026 barème and simple quotient-familial proxies: single = 1 part, married = 2 parts, singleWithDependents = 1.5 part. The décote, the full dependent-parts system, the plafonnement du quotient familial, and the automatic 10% abatements on salaries and pensions are documented in the gap report.

socialContributions.name: Cotisations retraite et vieillesse
socialContributions.tooltip: Grouped employee private-sector retirement bucket for 2026 salary income. It combines Assurance vieillesse plafonnée and déplafonnée with the standard Agirc-Arrco Tranche 1 and Tranche 2 rates, plus CEG and CET when annual salary exceeds the PASS. Public-sector, self-employed, cadre/APEC, reduced bases, and pension-income social levies are documented in the gap report.

additionalTaxes.name: CSG/CRDS sur revenus d'activité
additionalTaxes.tooltip: Employee-side CSG/CRDS approximation for 2026 salary income. FinSim applies the combined 9.7% rate to 98.25% of gross salary up to 4 PASS and to 100% beyond that threshold. Pension replacement rates, deductibility interactions, and RFR-based reduced rates are documented in the gap report.

capitalGainsTax.name: PFU
capitalGainsTax.tooltip: French taxable securities gains are modeled under the 2026 default prélèvement forfaitaire unique of 31.4% (12.8% income tax plus 18.6% social levies). The optional progressive-barème election and wrapper-specific regimes such as PEA and assurance-vie are documented in the gap report.

helpText.label: Plan d'épargne retraite (PER)
helpText.contributionDescriptionP1:
Enter how much of the modeled French PER deduction room you want to contribute from salary.

FinSim uses a simplified resident PER proxy equal to 10% of salary up to 8 times the 2026 PASS. The real French personalized ceiling uses prior-year professional income, minimum floors, carryforwards, and employer-plan offsets, which are documented in the gap report.
helpText.contributionDescriptionP2:
Enter how much of your partner's modeled French PER deduction room you want to contribute from salary.

FinSim applies the same simplified PER proxy to both people and does not model inter-year carryforwards or household reallocations.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP1:
Enter your expected monthly French state-pension amount.

Use your latest retirement estimate. FinSim applies a whole-year age schedule for the post-2023 legal-age transition and does not model quarter-count requirements or month-level cohort steps.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP2:
Enter your partner's expected monthly French state-pension amount.

Use their latest retirement estimate. FinSim applies the same whole-year age schedule for both people.

investmentTypes.label: Compte-titres ordinaire (actions / ETF)
investmentTypes.helpText: Taxable listed shares and ETFs held through an ordinary securities account are modeled under the 2026 default PFU rate of 31.4%. The optional progressive-barème election, the PEA regime, and plan-specific withholding details are not modeled separately.

investmentTypes.label: Compte-titres ordinaire (obligations / fonds obligataires)
investmentTypes.helpText: Taxable bonds and bond funds held through an ordinary securities account are modeled under the same 31.4% PFU-style disposal tax as other ordinary taxable wrappers. Product-specific withholding, accrual taxation, and assurance-vie mechanics are documented in the gap report.
